title: X-150 vs. Diesel: Transforming Mining Operations with Clean Syngas
author: Clemens Grosjean
excerpt: Mining operations across Africa spend millions on diesel generators. The X-150 offers a path to energy independence with 60% cost reduction and zero emissions.
content:
The Challenge: Diesel Dependency

Remote mining operations face a critical energy dilemma. Diesel generators, while reliable, come with significant drawbacks:
- Fuel costs: €0.25-0.35/kWh
- Supply chain vulnerability
- Environmental compliance pressure
- Carbon footprint reporting requirements

The Solution: Project MINEBASE

Zero-X Group has identified a €2.1 billion opportunity in African mining diesel replacement. Our X-150 systems convert on-site waste biomass into clean syngas, powering operations at a fraction of diesel costs.

Economic Analysis:

Diesel Generator vs X-150 System:
- Energy Cost: €0.30/kWh vs €0.08/kWh
- Fuel Transport: High vs None (on-site biomass)
- Emissions: 260g CO₂/kWh vs Carbon neutral
- Maintenance: Frequent vs Quarterly service

Case Study: West African Gold Mine

A mid-sized gold mining operation processing 2.5 million tonnes annually:
- Current diesel consumption: 45,000 L/month
- Diesel cost: €54,000/month
- X-150 solution: 3 units providing equivalent power
- Projected savings: €38,000/month (70% reduction)
- Payback period: 18 months

The MINEBASE Approach:

1. Site Assessment: Biomass availability analysis
2. System Design: X-150 configuration for specific power requirements
3. Build-Own-Operate: Zero-X installs, owns, and operates
4. Long-term Partnership: 15-year PPA with guaranteed savings

Environmental Impact:
- CO₂ reduction: 2,800 tonnes/year per site
- Waste valorization: 5,000 tonnes biomass/year
- No sulfur emissions
- Particulate-free syngas combustion

Next Steps:
Zero-X is actively engaging with mining operators in Ghana, Tanzania, and Zambia for pilot deployments in Q3 2026.

Contact: Jan von Rosenstiel, j.rosenstiel@mfc-energy.com
